factors.name: Micron earnings beat & AI memory demand
factors.summary: Micron crushed EPS ($25.11 vs $20.6 est), Q4 revenue guidance $31B vs $25.5B est, 86% gross margins. Signals AI-driven memory demand compounding faster than consensus models; stock ripped 15% AH.

factors.name: Qualcomm forward guidance & Meta partnership
factors.summary: Qualcomm nearly doubled 2029 non-handset revenue projection, stock +15% AH. Data center chips to generate 'billions' in 2027, reinforcing AI capex cycle acceleration.

factors.name: Broadcom custom chip for OpenAI
factors.summary: Broadcom unveiled custom AI chip for ChatGPT maker, directly challenging Nvidia's dominance and signaling sustained hyperscaler capex diversification into semiconductor ecosystem.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (DXY near 101.6)
factors.summary: USD Index at one-year high near 101.60, driven by higher-for-longer Fed expectations. Headwind for Nasdaq mega-caps with heavy international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Gold below $4000, real yields rising
factors.summary: Gold fell to 7-month low below $4000 on rising Fed hike bets; reflects elevated real yields pressuring long-duration growth equities and tech multiples.

factors.name: Fed rate hike speculation (Warsh, Bessent signals)
factors.summary: Treasury Secretary Bessent floated single 'tap the brakes' hike; NY Fed Marchioni cleanup language on reserves suggests potential policy shift. Market repricing higher-for-longer rates.

factors.name: JPMorgan flash crash warning on AI trades
factors.summary: JPMorgan lifted S&P 500 target to 7800 but explicitly warned of flash crash risk in crowded AI momentum names, suggesting easy money in second/third-order AI plays exhausted.

factors.name: South Korea Kospi +5%, Asia equities bid
factors.summary: Kospi surged >5% at open; US equity futures trading higher on Globex, reinforcing risk-on sentiment and positive momentum backdrop for Nasdaq.

factors.name: PCE inflation data pending (Thursday)
factors.summary: US PCE release imminent; high-impact event that could trigger Fed repricing. Markets are positioning defensively ahead, capping upside conviction.

factors.name: Oil prices down, Hormuz reopened
factors.summary: Crude oil ~$70.34, erasing wartime gains as Strait of Hormuz reopens and Iran peace talks progress. Lower energy inflation supports growth multiple stability.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is in early-stage rally mode following blockbuster Micron and Qualcomm AI earnings, with custom chip announcements from Broadcom adding to conviction that hyperscaler capex will sustain semiconductor demand. However, the move is being tempered by a surging US Dollar, rising real yields (gold below $4000), and Fed hike speculation, which are the primary headwinds for long-duration growth tech. JPMorgan's explicit flash crash warning on crowded AI trades and the looming PCE inflation release (Thursday) create near-term event risk that limits conviction. Asia opening strong (Kospi +5%) is positive momentum backdrop, but positioning suggests caution into macro data.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation (Thursday, high-impact)
Australian jobs report (June 25)
FOMC meeting outcomes (if scheduled)
Major mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN if in earnings season)
Cerebras further weakness if lock-up expires (already down 50% from highs)
Fed speakers on rate expectations
screening.risk_warning: If PCE surprises hot, Fed reprices hikes higher and real yields spike, triggering multiple compression and sector rotation out of mega-cap growth. JPMorgan's flash crash warning suggests late-stage retail euphoria could unwind violently. Dollar strength extending would also pressure international revenue streams. Move already underway (+0.38% 4h) — late entries risk mean-reversion into PCE data Thursday.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (benefiting from AH Micron/Qualcomm beats). Prudent entry on pullback to 29000–29150 support before PCE data; avoid chasing into resistance 29500+ ahead of Thursday inflation release.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Enter on current levels or minor dip to 29,000; semiconductor strength provides fresh catalyst after consolidation.

factors.name: Micron earnings blowout
factors.summary: Micron reported EPS $25.11 vs $20.60 est, guiding Q4 to $31 vs $25.50 est with ~86% gross margins on AI memory surge. Revenue beat by $5.7B with guidance topping by ~$7B at midpoint—stock up 15% after-hours, indicating AI capex acceleration is compounding faster than consensus models.

factors.name: Qualcomm guidance surge
factors.summary: Qualcomm almost doubled 2029 non-handset revenue projection and announced data center chips to produce 'billions' in '27, with stock popping 15%. Combined with Meta partnership news, this confirms AI infrastructure buildout extending beyond hyperscalers.

factors.name: Broad semiconductor rally
factors.summary: News headline states 'Micron and Qualcomm forecasts ignite $400 billion AI chip stock rally'—sector-wide AI demand confirmation lifting SMH/SOXX and core NDX semiconductor weighting.

factors.name: South Korea equities surge
factors.summary: Kospi up >5% at open with US equity index futures trading higher on Globex reopening, indicating strong risk-on sentiment in Asia that typically carries into US tech session.

factors.name: Fed policy expectations
factors.summary: Market positioning for higher-for-longer rates with DXY at one-year high near 101.60. NY Fed's Marchioni comments and Bessent floating single rate hike idea suggest policy may tighten further, pressuring long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Treasury yields and real rates
factors.summary: 10-year yields remain elevated with real rate pressures intact despite Wednesday slide. PCE data due Thursday could reprice Fed expectations; sustained high real yields compress growth stock valuations.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD Index at one-year high ~101.60, strengthening on higher-for-longer Fed outlook. NDX mega-caps have heavy international revenue exposure, making strong dollar a headwind to earnings translation.

factors.name: Bitcoin correlation breakdown
factors.summary: Bitcoin fell below $60,000 to 20-month lows with risk-off tone deepening. Historically correlated with tech/NDX on risk sentiment; crypto weakness suggests broader risk appetite concerns despite semiconductor strength.

factors.name: Oil collapse
factors.summary: Oil at $70.34, erasing most wartime gains on Hormuz reopening and Iran peace deal progress. Lower energy costs reduce inflation pressure (supportive) but also signal demand concerns and risk-off positioning (concerning).

factors.name: JPMorgan S&P 500 target raise with flash crash warning
factors.summary: JPM lifted S&P target to 7,800 but warned of flash crash risk in crowded AI trades, recommending barbell of quality growth and value. Suggests upside potential but increased volatility risk in speculative AI momentum names.

factors.name: VIX and risk sentiment
factors.summary: Mixed global equities, gold falling to 7-month lows, and haven demand for USD all point to elevated risk-off sentiment despite semiconductor optimism. Suggests bifurcated market with mega-cap tech diverging from broader risk assets.

factors.name: Asian futures positioning
factors.summary: Bloomberg Markets: 'Asian stocks were poised to gain, tracking a rally in US equity futures after Micron delivered upbeat forecast that reignited confidence in AI trade.' Positive overnight setup for US tech session.

factors.name: Bank stress test and capital returns
factors.summary: All 32 large banks passed Fed stress test; JPMorgan announced $50B buyback, Goldman raised dividend. Supports financial stability and liquidity but doesn't directly drive NDX components.

factors.name: Broadcom/OpenAI custom chip
factors.summary: Broadcom unveiled custom chip for OpenAI to support future AI models, challenging NVDA dominance. Diversification of AI chip supply chain reduces single-vendor risk and confirms infrastructure investment wave.

confirmation.narrative: NDX faces a bifurcated setup: massive semiconductor earnings beats from Micron (+15% AH) and Qualcomm (+15%) with $400B sector rally confirm AI infrastructure buildout is accelerating faster than Street models, providing fresh bullish catalyst. However, this occurs against a risk-off macro backdrop with DXY at 1-year highs, Bitcoin at 20-month lows, and elevated real rate pressures ahead of Thursday's PCE data. The -0.60% 24h decline represents consolidation/digestion rather than rejection, positioning for a gap-up on strong chip earnings.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data Thursday (Fed's preferred gauge—could reprice rate expectations)
Australia jobs report (risk sentiment indicator)
Semiconductor sector follow-through and conference commentary
Fed speaker commentary on rate path post-Bessent/Marchioni remarks
Asia session reaction to Micron/Qualcomm guidance
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if PCE comes in hot (>0.3% MoM core) triggering Fed hawkish repricing and real yield spike, or if semiconductor rally fades by Thursday open suggesting profit-taking overwhelms AI optimism. Break below 28,800 would signal broader risk-off taking control.

factors.name: Micron earnings & AI capex revived
factors.summary: Micron stock jumped 15% on gross margin expansion to 84.9% from 39% YoY, signaling memory shortage and strong AI demand. SK Hynix surged 12% announcing $29.4B US listing, confirming sustained semiconductor capex cycle.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain momentum
factors.summary: Kioxia planning US depositary offering in spring 2027; multiple chip majors signaling capacity expansion. Heavy NAS100 weighting in semis (NVDA, AMD, QCOM, LRCX, ASML) benefits from supply-constrained pricing environment.

factors.name: Fed rate hike expectations rising
factors.summary: Market pricing rising odds of September 2026 hike (first in 3 years) following Bessent 'tap the brakes' remark. Higher nominal rates compress long-duration tech multiples; USD strength on the move (DXY ~101.50).

factors.name: US PCE inflation print imminent (today 12:30 GMT)
factors.summary: Major catalyst hours away; market expects potential hot print given Fed tightening narrative. PCE surprise (either direction) could swing NAS100 50-100bp within minutes; confidence capped until release.

factors.name: Real yields rising, growth multiples under pressure
factors.summary: Gold falling below $4,000 for first time since November on Fed hike bets and stronger dollar; TIPS yields implicit in commodity weakness. Tech's long-duration cashflows increasingly vulnerable.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings cycle validation
factors.summary: No major NDX component earnings surprises reported in past 24h; Micron's success raises confidence in hyperscaler AI capex sustaining margins. Q2 earnings season momentum intact.

factors.name: Dollar strength and international headwinds
factors.summary: USD/JPY near 161.75 (intervention risk), EUR/USD ~1.1355 (lowest since June 2025). MSCI World ex-US at 25% P/E discount but flows remain stuck on US mega-caps; currency headwinds for NAS100 earnings translation.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium easing
factors.summary: Iran-US interim peace deal driving Hormuz tanker traffic resumption; oil erasing wartime gains (WTI ~$69.90). Lower risk premium supports risk-on equity appetite, though Venezuela earthquakes (magnitude 7.5) are isolated impact.

factors.name: AI regulation and IP concerns
factors.summary: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicit Claude model extraction; Alibaba shares slide to 16-month low. Growing regulatory scrutiny on IP/model theft and antitrust (EU DMA) adds tail risk to mega-cap cloud/AI revenue.

factors.name: Momentum and technicals
factors.summary: 4h change +0.38% vs 24h change -0.60% suggests intra-day bounce off overnight lows; no decisively extended move yet in either direction; retest of recent support/resistance pending.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two competing narratives: semiconductor earnings strength and AI capex momentum (Micron, SK Hynix, Kioxia) support a BUY into dips, but rising Fed rate hike odds (potentially September 2026, first in 3 years) and real yield expansion are compressing long-duration tech multiples. The US PCE print due in <3 hours is the immediate pivot point; a surprise hot print would accelerate the tightening repricing and likely trigger a sharp selloff, while a soft print could relieve pressure and allow the semiconductor/AI narrative to dominate. Current price action (small 4h gain vs 24h loss) suggests consolidation ahead of the print, not a conviction move yet.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation (May) release at 12:30 GMT today (2026-06-25) — consensus expects core PCE ~2.6% YoY; surprise either direction will reprice Fed hike odds and tech duration risk
FOMC minutes or Powell remarks in coming days — any hawkish signal amplifies rate hike narrative
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings (likely late June/early July) — AI capex commentary and margin guidance critical
10-year Treasury yield move (currently sensitive to real yield expectations) — watch for break above 4.30% as major headwind
Semiconductor equipment orders (ASML, LRCX, KLAC reports) — supply chain health check
screening.risk_warning: PCE surprise to the upside (hot) would invalidate the BUY bias and trigger rapid 200-300bp selloff in NAS100; alternatively, a sharp equity/credit event or China tariff escalation would override semiconductor momentum. Real yields breaking higher (10Y TIPS >1.5%) would accelerate multiple compression. Watch for Fed pivot reversal if economic data softens faster than expected.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (bounce underway intraday). Avoid aggressive long entries until after PCE release (too much event risk); consider adding longs on a post-PCE pullback to 29050–29100 support if print is soft, or wait for a retest of 29300 resistance if print is hot and triggers a brief spike-and-dump.

confirmation.entry_guidance: AI trade revival is underway; consider entries on pullbacks to 29,000-29,050 support rather than chasing current levels.

factors.summary: Bloomberg headline 'Micron Revives AI Trade' captures narrative shift. After weeks of debasement trade focus, capital rotating back into AI/semiconductor theme. Asia rally (Korea, Japan leading) suggests global risk-on in tech.

factors.name: Dollar weakness

factors.name: Real yields rising

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran/Hormuz)

factors.name: VIX/volatility environment
factors.summary: MUFG notes 'multi-year low volatility' in FX markets. No specific VIX/VXN levels cited but low vol typically supportive for equity risk-taking, though can reverse quickly on data surprises.

factors.name: Bitcoin/crypto correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin back above $60K, recouping losses as 'AI stocks stage rebound' per CoinDesk. Positive correlation intact but facing $10B options expiry headwinds. Mixed signal overall.

confirmation.narrative: Micron's blowout earnings (84.9% gross margin, +15% stock move) has catalyzed a sharp rotation back into the AI/semiconductor trade after weeks of dominance by the debasement/gold theme. Asia markets responded aggressively with SK Hynx +12% and Kospi +5%, signaling global appetite for tech risk. However, the move is already underway (Phase 2), and rising Fed rate hike expectations (Bessent's Greenspan analogy interpreted as September hike green light) plus today's imminent PCE data create meaningful cross-currents. Real yields rising (gold <$4,000) pressures long-duration tech multiples even as AI momentum returns.

factors.name: Micron earnings & chip rally
factors.summary: Micron delivered record quarter with strong forward guidance, sparking broad semiconductor rebound. SK Hynix +12% on US listing plans; Qualcomm, broader chip sector rallying premarket. FXStreet reports 'Micron sparks chip rally' and Nasdaq 100 futures up 2.25% to 30,180.

factors.name: AI capex & hyperscaler demand
factors.summary: Micron's upbeat revenue outlook reignites AI-driven growth narrative; Deutsche Bank notes chip strength revives AI rally momentum. No new capex cuts or demand warnings evident in news flow.

factors.name: PCE inflation data (imminent)
factors.summary: US core PCE for May due Thursday 12:30 GMT; markets pricing first Fed hike in September. Expectation is data-dependent; a cool read would extend equity rally, hot read could temper gains. High volatility risk into release.

factors.name: Fed rate expectations
factors.summary: Markets are now pricing September Fed rate hike after hawkish recent commentary; ECB policymaker Schnabel reiterates further tightening likely needed. Rising real yields/terminal rate expectations typically compress tech multiple expansion.

factors.name: US Dollar strength
factors.summary: DXY holding above 101.50, USD on fourth consecutive winning day. Strong dollar headwind for USD-denominated Nasdaq 100 revenue from international operations; limits multiple expansion.

factors.name: Real yields (implicit)
factors.summary: Gold has broken below $4,000 and silver down 50% from highs; BNY Mellon cites 'rising global front-end yields and fading debasement fears' as pressure. Higher real rates typically compress long-duration growth stock multiples.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: 4h change +0.38%, but 24h change still -0.60% showing intraday bounce into tech strength. Nasdaq 100 futures spiked 2.25% on Micron overnight; current spot up modestly suggests move is in early-to-mid phase.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings calendar
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings imminent in next 24-72h visible in news. Micron's blowout was standalone catalyst; waiting for FAANG earnings cycle updates.

factors.name: FTSE Russell reconstitution Friday
factors.summary: Semi-annual rebalancing Friday flagged as 'one of biggest volume days of year.' Potential for elevated volatility and option-driven price discovery, but directional bias unclear.

factors.name: Geopolitical: Israel-Lebanon, Iran, Hormuz
factors.summary: Israel pullback from Lebanon buffer zone described as 'good faith'; tanker traffic resuming from Persian Gulf; oil prices erasing wartime gains. De-escalation narrative broadly positive for growth equities and risk-on sentiment.

screening.narrative: Micron's record earnings and upbeat AI capex guidance triggered a sharp overnight rally in Nasdaq 100 futures (+2.25% to 30,180), lifting the complex on renewed conviction in AI-driven demand and semiconductor supply. However, the broader macro backdrop remains headwind-laden: Fed rate hike expectations have shifted to September, real yields are rising (evidenced by gold/silver collapse), and USD strength continues to weigh on international revenue multiples. The equity market is now in a tug-of-war between tech earnings strength (Micron, chip sector) and macro headwinds (rates, dollar, geopolitical tail risks evaporating into oil lows). Current spot at 29,220 is +0.38% on the day but still -0.60% on 24h, suggesting the intraday rally is partially reversing overnight gains—a Phase 2 consolidation into the Friday reconstitution and Thursday PCE release.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation (core & headline) Thursday 12:30 GMT — critical for Fed repricing
US Initial Jobless Claims Thursday morning — labor market softness could support no-hike narrative
FTSE Russell reconstitution Friday — potential vol spike and option-driven rebalancing flows
FOMC summary of economic projections (if any commentary due) — key for terminal rate guidance
Semiconductor earnings follow-ups (Taiwan-listed TSMC, Samsung) — validate Micron momentum or signal cycle peak
screening.risk_warning: PCE inflation surprise to the upside (>0.4% m/m or >3.1% y/y core) would crush the Micron rally and trigger Fed tightening repricing, likely selling off NQ 1-2%. Conversely, any additional geopolitical escalation (Iran intervention, Hormuz closure) would reverse oil de-escalation gains and restore inflation/stagflation fears. Strong dollar persistence (DXY >102) could cap upside if earnings growth in international operations disappoints. Late-stage move into resistance 30,180 with Friday reconstitution vol overhead argues for caution—consider waiting for retest/pullback entry rather than chasing spot rally.
screening.entry_guidance: Micron-led chip rally is real but move is in Phase 2 (already up 2.25% futures overnight). Recommend waiting for pullback to 29,500–29,800 support before adding longs; avoid chasing spot highs into Friday vol event and Thursday PCE release. Short-term traders should scale out at 30,180 resistance; longer-term longs can hold through PCE if headline/core comes in cool (<2.7% core y/y).

factors.name: Micron earnings catalyst

factors.summary: Core PCE for May due at 12:30 GMT Thursday is expected to show inflation advance. Markets are in wait-and-see mode; cooler-than-expected reading could ease Fed tightening concerns and boost tech, hotter reading reinforces rate hike path.

confirmation.narrative: Micron's blowout earnings and AI-optimistic guidance have catalyzed a powerful semiconductor-led rally in NQ futures (+2.25%), reversing recent weakness and reigniting confidence in the AI capex cycle. However, this bullish impulse collides with a persistent Fed hawkish backdrop—markets now price September rate hikes, real yields remain elevated, and core PCE data due in hours could either validate or challenge this rebound. The tech rally appears genuine (not just surface-level) with SK Hynix, Qualcomm, and chip stocks broadly participating, but the macro overhang of rising rates and dollar strength tempers conviction.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Micron-driven rally is well underway with NQ futures already up 2.25%; consider waiting for PCE data release or a pullback toward 29,000-29,100 support zone before adding long exposure rather than chasing current levels.

The PCE release at 12:30 GMT today is a critical juncture; a hot reading could derail the tech relief rally and force a retest of recent lows. factors.summary: Hawkish Fed repricing has pushed real yields higher, creating compression pressure on high-multiple growth stocks. However, news of oil price collapse (Brent down to pre-war $72) may ease inflation premia and cap yield upside.

factors.name: Oil price collapse (Strait of Hormuz reopening)
factors.summary: Brent crude fell to ~$72 (pre-Iran war levels) as Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes and Saudi Arabia restarts Ras Tanura exports. Lower energy costs ease inflation fears and may support dovish PCE interpretation, reducing stagflation risk for tech.

factors.name: USD strength
factors.summary: USD continues to outperform on Fed hawkishness (USD/JPY at 1986 highs near 162.00, USD/CHF at 10-month high). NDX components have heavy international revenue exposure, making strong dollar a headwind to earnings.

factors.name: VIX & risk sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 remains under pressure per news, retail investors reducing leverage/options exposure (JPMorgan), and Bitcoin derivatives signal panic. However, Micron earnings triggered risk-on rebound in tech futures specifically.

factors.name: Defensive rotation warnings
factors.summary: BofA's Raedler advises switching to defensives 'if AI trade falters,' and Citrini Research warns AI trade is 'getting crowded.' This indicates institutional caution even as Micron provided near-term relief.

factors.name: QQQ/tech ETF flows & positioning
factors.summary: No specific flow data in news, but Russell rebalancing Friday (noted as 'one of biggest volume days') could create technical volatility. Retail leverage reduction suggests less fuel for momentum chase.

factors.name: Upcoming catalysts: PCE & FOMC
factors.summary: US core PCE data drops in ~1 hour (12:30 GMT). Hot print would reinforce Fed hike path and pressure tech multiples despite Micron optimism. Markets also pricing in Fed tightening bias with next FOMC decision critical.

confirmation.narrative: Micron's blowout earnings and strong AI demand guidance triggered a sharp 2% rally in Nasdaq futures, directly addressing the 'AI jitters' that weighed on tech this week. However, the move is already underway in pre-market, placing this in Phase 2. The bullish catalyst faces meaningful headwinds: core PCE data due in ~1 hour could reinforce the Fed's hawkish tilt (36 bps of tightening priced by year-end), and sustained USD strength pressures international revenues. Oil's collapse to $72 provides a counterbalancing disinflationary tailwind. The setup favors continuation of the relief rally but with elevated risk around the PCE print—hot inflation data could quickly reverse gains.
catalysts_ahead: US Core PCE Price Index (May) - due 12:30 GMT today - consensus expecting uptick
Russell 2000 rebalancing Friday - high volume technical event
Fed speakers post-PCE interpreting data
Next FOMC decision - 34% probability of rate hike priced in
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if core PCE comes in significantly hot (>0.3% MoM), triggering Fed hike repricing and erasing Micron-driven gains. Also vulnerable if USD strength accelerates or if institutional rotation to defensives (per BofA warning) gains momentum. Entry at current levels is late—chasing the 2% futures gap-up carries elevated risk into the PCE print.

factors.name: Micron Earnings Blowout
factors.summary: Micron crushed Q2 earnings with revenue surging from $9.3B to $41.46B y/y, raising guidance well above estimates. Stock up 18%, leading tech rally and easing AI capex slowdown fears.

factors.name: PCE Inflation In-Line (Dovish Reprieve)
factors.summary: US PCE rose 4.1% y/y as expected; Core PCE 3.4% matched forecast. No acceleration surprise, easing near-term Fed hike repricing fears and supporting long-duration growth multiple expansion.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Compression
factors.summary: 10-year yield down 2.5bps to 4.374%, 2-year down 3bps to 4.106% post-data. Lower nominal yields directly support Nasdaq 100's high-beta growth names and long-duration valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields Moderating
factors.summary: With nominal yields falling on in-line PCE and real yields compressing, conditions favor large-cap tech. Q1 GDP revised up to 2.1% vs 1.6% expected adds growth backdrop.

factors.name: Semiconductor Demand Strength
factors.summary: Micron beat confirms memory-chip demand remains robust despite pricing pressures; MSTR/Strategy up on macro-backdrop normalization. AI capex cycle remains intact, benefiting NVDA, ASML, SMH complex.

factors.name: Apple Price Hike on Memory Shortage
factors.summary: Apple raised Mac/iPad prices due to memory crunch; stock dropped 5% on margin concern. Signals potential supply-chain stress and pricing power limits in consumer tech; drag on consumer discretionary.

factors.name: Bitcoin Flash Crash ($61K→$58K) & MSTR Weakness
factors.summary: BTC plunged 3K on overnight sell-off, testing support. MSTR/STRC hit 52-week lows, indicating crypto-risk sentiment deterioration and potential correlation drag on Nasdaq growth on risk-off days.

factors.name: Dollar Strength Moderating
factors.summary: USD pulled back from highs post-PCE; yen intervention risks cited (JPY shorts stretched). Weaker dollar aids US tech earnings translation but removes near-term commodity headwind.

factors.name: Tech Earnings Calendar Momentum
factors.summary: Micron blockbuster broke earnings recession fears mid-cycle; Q2 tech earnings season remains supportive. Mega-cap FAANG names (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA) due in coming weeks will set tone.

factors.name: Valuation Sensitivity to Real Yields
factors.summary: Long-duration growth stocks (NVDA, META, TSLA, MSFT) extremely sensitive to real rate moves; PCE hold and yield compression create tailwind for multiple expansion if trend continues.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Venezuela, Iran, Hormuz)
factors.summary: Venezuelan earthquakes (164+ dead), Iran–US tensions over Hormuz, Pakistan peacemaking efforts. Oil down on supply normalization, reducing inflation tail-risk but geopolitical volatility remains latent.

factors.name: Meme-Stock Contagion (Wendy's Rally Fades)
factors.summary: Wendy's meme surge failed to extend second day, signaling waning retail euphoria. Limited systemic risk but sentiment fragility warrant caution on extended rallies in frothy sectors.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 broke higher on Micron's blockbuster earnings and benign PCE inflation, easing Fed hawkishness fears and compressing real yields — a classic bullish confluence for long-duration tech. Q1 GDP revised to 2.1% and jobless claims at 15-year lows provide growth cushion. However, Bitcoin's overnight flash crash and MSTR/Strategy weakness signal latent risk-off vulnerability, and Apple's 5% drop on pricing concerns tempers euphoria. The move is ~1–1.5% intraday (Phase 2), so conviction should remain measured; retest support critical before chasing.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC rate decision (late June or early July, monitor schedule)
Mega-cap earnings: NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META (next 2–4 weeks)
CPI surprise (next scheduled CPI release ~ early July)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (end June/early July)
NFP (first Friday of July)
Apple guidance reset post-price-hike (July earnings)
Federal Reserve communications and 10-year yield repricing
screening.risk_warning: Real yields spike on inflation surprise or hawkish Fed pivot → NQ multiple compression and long-duration names sell off hard. Apple margin crisis spreads to other consumer tech. Bitcoin continues break below $58K, triggering crypto-correlation cascade into equities. China-Taiwan escalation or chip export controls → NVDA/ASML crater. Earnings misses from NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL on AI capex slowdown → growth multiple unwind. Move already 1–1.5% in 2h — retest or consolidation before new highs is higher-probability entry.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already in progress (Phase 2); best entry is retest of support at 19350–19400 on 2–5min consolidation or pullback. If NAS100 breaks through 19800 on volume, next target 20000+, but chase-rally risk high — wait for pullback confirmation.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Wait for post-data digestion; Micron rally offset by Apple price-hike selloff creates cross-currents. Await clear direction after AAPL stabilization or further semiconductor confirmation.

factors.name: Micron earnings blowout
factors.summary: Micron beat estimates massively, revenue quadrupled Y/Y to $41.46B, stock up 10-18% (reports vary), near all-time highs. This validates AI infrastructure demand and semiconductor cycle strength, directly bullish for NDX tech weighting.

factors.name: Apple price hikes on memory crunch
factors.summary: Apple raised prices on MacBooks, iPads, and other devices Thursday morning due to memory chip shortage, stock down ~5%. AAPL is ~12% of NDX weighting, creating significant drag that offsets Micron gains.

factors.name: PCE inflation in-line
factors.summary: May PCE came in at 4.1% Y/Y vs 4.1% expected, core PCE 3.4% vs 3.4% expected (both per US BEA). No surprise means no re-pricing of Fed expectations; markets relieved but not catalyzed.

factors.name: Fed rate expectations
factors.summary: Multiple sources note markets now pricing Fed hikes into 2026 despite in-line PCE. ABN AMRO argues repricing has gone too far, but elevated rate expectations compress long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Treasury yields
factors.summary: 10-year yield at 4.374%, down only 2.5 bps post-PCE; 2-year at 4.106% down 3 bps. Yields remain elevated, pressuring growth stock valuations despite modest post-data relief.

factors.name: Microsoft deeply oversold
factors.summary: News mentions MSFT RSI at 23.97, deeply oversold with bearish trend. Oversold conditions on a mega-cap can signal near-term bounce opportunity, supportive for NDX.

factors.name: Tesla steep downtrend
factors.summary: TSLA described as 'in steep downtrend, RSI near oversold.' While oversold can mean bounce, persistent downtrend in a top-10 NDX component is a drag.

factors.name: Bitcoin flash crash
factors.summary: BTC dropped from $61k to $58k in sharp sell-off (Bitcoin Magazine), with Strategy MSTR/STRC hitting new lows. Crypto weakness often correlates with risk-off sentiment in tech, especially high-beta NDX names.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD rally extends as Fed repricing dominates (ABN AMRO), though modestly lower post-PCE. Stronger USD is headwind for NDX components with heavy international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Meta vs Micron dynamics
factors.summary: Micron just passed Meta in market cap for first time; analysis notes Micron is gouging Meta and others on memory pricing. This is a zero-sum dynamic within tech—Micron gains are costs for hyperscalers, potentially pressuring their margins.

factors.name: AI infrastructure demand
factors.summary: Micron's blowout report and guidance validate relentless AI infrastructure demand. Article notes 'Micron overtakes Meta, Tesla in market value amid relentless AI infrastructure demand,' confirming secular AI capex cycle intact.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply constraints
factors.summary: Memory crunch forcing Apple price hikes is bullish for chip makers (Micron) but bearish for device OEMs and consumer demand. Constraint-driven price increases can dampen end-market demand, hurting broader tech.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 faces conflicting forces Thursday: Micron's monster earnings (revenue up 4x, stock +10-18%) validates AI infrastructure demand and ignited a semiconductor rally, but Apple's 5% drop on memory-crunch-driven price hikes offsets gains given AAPL's ~12% NDX weight. PCE inflation came in exactly as expected (4.1% headline, 3.4% core), providing no new catalyst but keeping elevated Fed hike expectations intact. Treasury yields remain high (10Y at 4.374%), compressing long-duration tech multiples, while Bitcoin's flash crash to $58k and broader crypto weakness signal risk-off sentiment. The divergence between semiconductor strength (Micron, supply-side winners) and mega-cap device OEMs (Apple, cost pressure) creates cross-currents. Prior bias history shows consistent BUY 7/10 readings, but today's Apple drag and mixed signals warrant caution—this is not a clean continuation setup.
catalysts_ahead: Friday: any follow-through on Micron guidance and Apple price-hike impact on demand outlook
Next week: month-end rebalancing flows, any Fed speaker commentary on PCE data
Upcoming: NVDA earnings (critical AI demand confirmation), next FOMC meeting, July CPI/PCE releases
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if Apple selloff deepens (undermining device/consumer tech sector) or if semiconductor rally proves isolated without broader mega-cap participation. Elevated yields combined with crypto weakness and potential demand destruction from Apple price hikes could trigger broader tech de-rating. Also, if Fed speakers turn more hawkish post-PCE despite in-line data, rate expectations could push higher and compress NDX multiples further.

factors.name: Real yields (TIPS direction)
factors.summary: Treasury auction yield 4.26% with C- grade; higher nominal yields + sticky inflation = real yields likely elevated, compressing Nasdaq valuations on long-duration growth names.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings scatter

factors.name: Sector divergence (Micron vs mega-cap)
factors.summary: Micron revenue +330% YoY with stellar guidance, but that's gouging downstream tech customers (Meta, Apple) on memory pricing. Micron now briefly > Meta market cap—structural margin pressure for NDX giants, not a bullish sign.

factors.name: Geopolitical volatility (Iran/Hormuz, Venezuela)
factors.summary: Iran tensions re-escalating with ship attacks in Strait of Hormuz; Venezuela earthquakes create uncertainty. Oil volatility can create chop in sentiment but no clear directional bias yet for tech.

factors.name: Dollar weakness post-PCE
factors.summary: USD pulled back despite hot inflation; EUR/USD near 1.1380, GBP/USD, CAD/USD all firmer. Weaker dollar helps NDX international revenue stream but contradicts risk-off sentiment.

factors.name: European equities record close
factors.summary: European shares hit record with healthcare leading; suggests some risk-on mood globally, though NDX lagging despite this.

factors.name: Crypto crash (BTC to $58K, -20% monthly)
factors.summary: Bitcoin plunged to 21-month lows; $600M hourly crypto liquidations signal risk-off contagion. NQ-BTC linkage suggests weakness in speculative positioning and retail momentum.

factors.name: Bias history shift
factors.summary: Prior two reads (08:59, 11:45) were BUY 7/10; 14:29 read (Sonnet) dropped to NEUTRAL 5/10. This fresh downgrade reflects intraday repricing on inflation data and mega-cap earnings disappointment.

factors.name: Earnings surprise: Apple price hikes vs margin compression risk
factors.summary: Apple forced memory cost pass-through (6% decline) signals consumer demand resilience but margin erosion. Suggests stagflationary backdrop where growth is muted and pricing power is squeezed.

key_levels.resistance: 29600–29700 (daily high area; overbought if Micron euphoria fades)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a multi-speed earnings cycle: Micron's blockbuster results (+16%) lifted semiconductors and AI infrastructure sentiment, but mega-cap tech (Apple, NVIDIA, Meta, MSFT) are showing fatigue amid sticky PCE inflation (3-year high), margin pressure from memory costs, and higher real yields. The +0.53% 4h/24h move is modest and contradicts the underlying weakness in NDX heavyweights. Cryptocurrency contagion (Bitcoin -$3K to 21-month lows) and geopolitical risk (Iran/Hormuz, Venezuela) add chop. The prior BUY bias has been downgraded to NEUTRAL as inflation repricing and mega-cap earnings disappointment offset Micron's tailwind.

NVIDIA, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Tesla earnings (if not yet reported)

screening.risk_warning: Bias could flip BULLISH if: (1) mega-cap earnings beat and raise guidance (esp. MSFT, GOOGL cloud capex), (2) PCE reverses lower in next week, (3) Fed signals patience/cuts, (4) oil stabilizes/geopolitical risk recedes, or (5) real yields decline sharply. Conversely, bias to SELL if mega-cap earnings deteriorate further, inflation stays sticky, or crypto contagion spreads to equities via margin calls.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early PHASE 2 with only +0.53% progress; do not chase. Wait for retest of 29300 support or break below 29200 before committing short. If 10-year yield breaks above 4.30%, consider tactical short. For longs, require Nasdaq 100 to clear 29700 and hold with expanding breadth across mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA).

factors.name: Mega-cap Tech Fatigue
factors.summary: NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META, TSLA all described as 'looking tired and old' with NVDA below 100-day MA at $196.17. AAPL raising prices signals sticky inflation concerns, not growth optimism.

factors.name: Micron Semiconductor Strength
factors.summary: MSTR crashed ~10% on lawsuit/BTC weakness, but MSTR overtook META and TSLA in market value on 'relentless AI infrastructure demand.' Micron earnings stellar, hitting new ATHs—bullish for chip cycle and hyperscaler capex.

factors.name: PCE Inflation & Fed Messaging
factors.summary: Core PCE came in hot (faster than 3yr+ pace per MarketWatch). Fed's Goolsbee: 'inflation is too high' and 'going the wrong way'; Williams pushes 2% target to 2028 from 2027. No rate cuts imminent—growth multiples under pressure.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Sticky inflation + Fed hold stance = higher real yields. Long-duration growth tech (NDX core) extremely sensitive; 10yr Treasury volatility expected to climb per T. Rowe Price commentary.

factors.name: Bifurcated Market Rotation
factors.summary: Mega-caps bleeding while smaller cap/value rallying. Dow hit record while NAS100 flat. Tech concentration risk rising; breadth weakening. Classic sign of narrative shift away from mega-cap growth dominance.

factors.name: Crypto Contagion & Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: BTC crashed to $58K (21-month low), MSTR lawsuit-hit. Crypto weakness typically precedes broader risk-off in growth/tech. NAS100-BTC correlation remains intact on vol days.

factors.name: Geopolitical Oil Risk (Strait of Hormuz)
factors.summary: Iran attack on cargo ship raising oil prices (+2.25% WTI) and inflation expectations. If crude spikes further, CPI surprise risks materialize, hitting growth multiple compression harder.

factors.name: Space Stock FOMO Ending
factors.summary: SpaceX FOMO 'officially over,' space stocks 'getting punished' across the board. Early warning sign that high-flying, unprofitable growth darlings losing favor; may signal broader multiple reset.

factors.name: Bond ETF Flows & Yield-Hunting
factors.summary: Bond investors abandoning aggregate benchmarks and 'hunting yield'—classic defensive repositioning. Suggests rotation out of duration/growth into income, bearish for high-multiple NAS100 names.

factors.name: Recent Price Action: Flat, Choppy
factors.summary: 4h change -0.27%, 24h +0.29%. No directional conviction; oscillating around unchanged. Move is in early Phase 2, choppy, no breakout yet in either direction.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two opposing forces: (1) lingering AI/semiconductor bullish narrative (Micron strength, hyperscaler capex), and (2) deteriorating Fed backdrop (sticky inflation, higher real yields, no rate-cut relief) combined with mega-cap tech exhaustion (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT all fatigued). The market is rotating away from concentration in mega-caps toward smaller-cap value and income assets. Geopolitical oil spike risks add a stagflation tail risk. Overall: choppy consolidation with downside bias if inflation data surprises hot again or if big-tech earnings disappoint.

Mega-cap earnings: MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA detailed AI capex commentary

Bias flips BEARISH if: (a) CPI/PCE surprise hot again; (b) mega-cap earnings miss and guide lower; (c) Fed commits to 'higher for longer'; (d) real yields spike >1% on term premium reset.
screening.entry_guidance: NAS100 is choppy and in early Phase 2. Avoid chasing; wait for a decisive break (>0.5%) above 29,700 resistance before considering long, OR wait for pullback to 29,100 support on weakness before testing reversal. Better to wait for next major catalyst (earnings or CPI) to clarify directional bias. Current risk/reward unfavorable due to sticky inflation backdrop and mega-cap fatigue.

factors.name: Mega-cap Tech Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: Apple slammed on price hikes amid chip memory constraints; Microsoft suffering 'historic June rout' as investors balk at heavy capex spending. Palantir down >30% in June, hitting 52-week lows. Single-name weakness is dragging the index.

factors.name: PCE Inflation Data (May)
factors.summary: PCE came in at 4.1% vs 4.1% expected (no surprise), but core PCE remains sticky. Dallas Fed trimmed mean PCE at +2.8% vs +2.4% prior signals persistent services inflation. Fed officials (Goolsbee, Williams) both emphasize inflation is 'going the wrong way' and pushed 2% target to 2028.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: NY Fed President Williams delayed 2% inflation return to 2028 (from 2027 expectation), stating monetary policy remains 'well positioned' for 'higher-for-longer.' Goolsbee: 'inflation side of mandate is clearly the problem.' Rate cut expectations receding; growth multiples under pressure.

factors.name: Semiconductor Demand & Supply Chain
factors.summary: Micron earnings 'blowout forecast' reignited AI trade; On Semiconductor announced $7B Synaptics acquisition with $30B TAM bump. Apple/Microsoft raising prices on chips. Qualcomm chasing $40B transformation with Meta already buying in. Strong capex momentum from hyperscalers.

factors.name: US Dollar & Real Yields
factors.summary: USD Index eased to 101.40 after probing 101.75 despite firm PCE data; Dollar rally stalling. Real yields (TIPS proxied by sticky inflation) remain elevated, pressuring long-duration growth (Nasdaq constituents). Ambiguous directional signal.

factors.name: Mega-cap Tech Technicals
factors.summary: News: 'Price action in tech stocks is a mess.' All of NVDA, GOOGL, META, MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, TSLA 'looking tired.' Bifurcated market with largest companies struggling while smaller ones climb. S&P 500 'at critical crossroads' with break lower signaling more losses ahead.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Outlook
factors.summary: Micron forecast bump, On Semi/Synaptics deal, US $250M I-Pulse semiconductor investment signal sustained AI infrastructure spending. Forward guidance from cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) not yet revised down; capex commitment appears intact despite expense concerns.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Middle East)
factors.summary: Iran fired on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz, UN halted evacuation. Oil prices bounced (+2.25% to $71.92) but remain below 200-day MA. Supply disruption risk reignited, inflationary pressures on commodities, potential margin squeeze for tech.

factors.name: VIX & Options Positioning
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in news, but Bitcoin options traders are hedging downside (Anchorage report), suggesting elevated tail-risk premium. Nasdaq vol (VXN) likely elevated given mega-cap selloff and lack of directional conviction.

factors.name: Crypto/Bitcoin Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin crashed to $58K–$59K, hit new 2026 lows, Strategy (MSTR) down ~10% with securities lawsuit landing. BTC power-law model frames crash as 'normal' but bear flag breakdown suggests next target $54K or lower. Risk-off correlation intact.

factors.name: ETF Flows & Sentiment
factors.summary: No explicit QQQ/XLK flow data in news. However, bifurcated market (mega-caps down, small-caps up) suggests possible rotation out of large-cap growth and into value/cyclicals. Meme rally (Wendy's) failed to extend, indicating retail capitulation risk.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts
factors.summary: Japan inflation (Tokyo CPI) due Friday June 26; expected to accelerate to 1.6% core YoY (vs 1.3% prior) on energy/supply concerns. No major US macro (no FOMC/CPI/NFP in immediate 24-48h window). Earnings calendar light in near term.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a PHASE_2 downside move driven by mega-cap tech underperformance (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META all struggling), persistent inflation (PCE 4.1%, core sticky, services inflating), and Fed officials pushing back rate-cut expectations to 2028. While semiconductor demand (Micron, On Semi, Qualcomm) and hyperscaler capex intentions remain constructive, these positives are being overwhelmed by (1) multiple compression from higher-for-longer rates, (2) single-name tech earnings misses and capex concerns, and (3) risk-off sentiment (Bitcoin -10%, Strategy -10%, options hedging). Middle East tensions add commodity tail risk. The market is bifurcated: large-cap growth bleeding, value/cyclicals and small-caps outperforming. No major US catalyst in next 24h (Japan CPI minor); market poised to re-test support on lack of conviction.
catalysts_ahead: Japan Tokyo CPI (core) June 26 – expected to accelerate to 1.6% YoY; could signal global inflation stickiness
Fed speakers (Goolsbee, Williams remarks ongoing) – both already hawkish; unlikely to spark rally

FOMC minutes or speaker calendar – check for dovish surprises (low probability)

DOWNSIDE INVALIDATION: If major tech earnings prove resilient, capex commentary remains upbeat, and inflation surprise to downside, bias flips bullish. Currently, move down is partially extended (4h -0.07% is modest, but 24h +0.49% and prior multi-day weakness suggest momentum is downside); a retest of 29200–29300 would offer higher-probability entry for either direction.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in mid-phase; do not chase either direction into the close. Wait for retest of support 29200–29300 for a potential reversal bounce setup, or break below with conviction and volume for a lower-target entry. Monitor Japan CPI (Fri) and any pre-market Fed speaker comments for intraday direction clue.

factors.name: US PCE Inflation (May)

factors.name: US GDP (Q1 Final)
factors.summary: GDP revised to +2.1% vs +1.6% estimate, showing economic resilience. Jobless claims 215K vs 225K expected — labor market holding up.

factors.name: Real Yields / TIPS Pressure

factors.summary: Micron delivered 'blowout' earnings and AI demand commentary, triggering rebound in semiconductors (NVDA, SMH rallied). But MSFT stock 'suffering historic June rout' due to heavy capex spending concerns; AAPL slammed on price hikes (+$150-200 on MacBooks/iPads). factors.summary: All of NVDA, GOOGL, META, MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, TSLA 'looking tired' per technical analysis. MSFT capex burn and AAPL price hike headwinds suggest margin/growth concerns. Heavy international revenue exposure facing USD strength (DXY near 101.75 earlier, settled ~101.45).

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY at multi-month highs near 101.75, but eased post-PCE data. Stronger USD typically headwind for US large-cap tech with ~30-40% international revenue; modest offset by commodities weakness (oil $71.92).

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Fed speakers (Goolsbee, Williams) signal 'no hurry to move in either direction' but emphasize sticky inflation risk and upside bias to rate path. Market pricing near-term easing faces direct pushback. Higher-for-longer posture confirmed.

factors.summary: Micron earnings 'hard to overstate' in importance per Mizuho; $7B ON Semi–Synaptics deal signals continued AI capex momentum. CHIPS awards (I-Pulse $250M) and supply chain diversification support long-term sector health.

factors.summary: Iran attack on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz renewed transit risks, but oil recovered only moderately (+2.25% to $71.92). Risk priced but not elevated; crude still below 200-day MA at $73.77. Low systemic equity impact so far.

factors.summary: VIX/VXN not explicitly mentioned in high-conviction terms. Bitcoin options show defensive hedging (downside protection), suggesting broader risk-off tilt. Single-name gamma in mega-cap tech mentioned as 'tired' but no extremes flagged.

factors.name: Market Breadth & Rotation
factors.summary: Dow outperformed (+0.49% for NQ per context, but S&P flat, Dow higher). 'Completely bifurcated market' with mega-caps struggling and smaller caps rallying. Concentration risk in largest companies is a structural headwind for NDX.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts (72h)
factors.summary: Japan CPI data Friday morning (inflation expected to accelerate to 1.6% core from 1.3%), may pressure BoJ rate hike bets. No major US economic data or earnings in immediate 24-48h window; earnings season tapering. Fed speakers will continue to dominate tone.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught in a classic tug-of-war: robust Q1 GDP (+2.1% beat) and resilient labor data support equities, but sticky PCE (4.1% vs 4.1%, with services weakness acknowledged by Goolsbee) and Williams' hawkish surprise (2% inflation target pushed to 2028) keep real yields elevated and duration-sensitive tech under pressure. Mega-cap earnings paint a mixed picture—Micron's AI demand upside offset by MSFT's capex burn concerns and AAPL's margin headwinds from supply-chain cost pass-throughs. The market is bifurcated: semiconductors rebounding on AI narrative, but the largest tech stocks ('NVDA, GOOGL, META, MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, TSLA all looking tired') facing multiple compression. USD strength near 101.75 adds headwind to international revenue streams. Three consecutive NEUTRAL reads suggest no fresh confirmation in either direction—a consolidation phase.
catalysts_ahead: Japan CPI (Friday 26 Jun AM) — expected to accelerate core to 1.6% from 1.3%, may refresh BoJ rate hike debate and impact risk sentiment
Fed speakers (continuing commentary from Goolsbee, Williams on inflation trajectory and rate path)
Tech earnings trickle (Mag 7 already largely reported; watch for guidance revisions and capex commentary)
OpenAI IPO timing (NYT: leaning 2027, not imminent, but could drive AI/growth narrative shifts)
China tensions update (tech ban escalation risk, esp. semiconductors and EV supply chains)
screening.risk_warning: A Fed pivot to dovish messaging (inflation showing clearer disinflation path, real yields falling significantly) or a major mega-cap earnings beat (e.g., hyperscaler cloud capex slowdown claim correction) could flip bias decisively BULLISH. Conversely, a CPI or PCE surprise to the upside, or geopolitical escalation (Iran/Hormuz disrupting oil and supply chains), could reignite risk-off and push NAS100 lower past 29,000. The bifurcated market (mega-cap struggles, small-cap rally) suggests rotation risk—if consolidation breaks, directional conviction will follow breadth.
screening.entry_guidance: Move partially underway (slight 4h decline, but +0.49% 24h suggests no extended bearish cascade yet). Recommend waiting for retest of 29,600 resistance (potential short entry on failure) or a clear break below 29,200 support (signal of Phase 3 downtrend). NEUTRAL bias does not warrant fresh directional entry until Fed tone clarifies or earnings surprise breaks the stalemate.
